column5: Notes
Frames deal context and stage logic. Stage is descriptive only; do not infer deal quality or forecast.
Defines time horizon for planning. Raw date only; urgency or risk are evaluated downstream.
Deal size. Used as factual input for risk, scrutiny, and resourcing models (evaluated downstream).
Roles involved in evaluation. Input is factual; downstream logic will determine influence and alignment.

column2: Enablement, Speed, Risk Mitigation, Efficiency, Transformation
column5: Primary business or technical pain or objective as stated by the buyer. Raw user input only.
Observable champion behaviors (selling on our behalf, coaching, information share, intros, partnering). Do not infer strength—measured in Champion Strength lever.
Whether EB alignment and support is confirmed. Binary input; strength is evaluated downstream.

column2: Security exposure, Cost savings disappearing, Regulatory requirement start date, Finance milestone, Purchase before budget freeze, Seasonality
column5: External or internal trigger that affects timing. Input only—urgency interpretation happens in levers layer.

column2: None, Deadline, Project start, Audit date, Renewal date, Budget cycle, Commercial term
column5: Stated timeline, deadline, or term requirement. Raw data only.

column2: RFP, Discovery, Demos, Preliminary pricing, POC/Pilot, Workshop, Security review, Finance approval, Legal review, Negotiation
column5: Known steps, timelines, and people in the buyer decision process. Capture only what is explicitly known; do not infer clarity or completeness.

column2: Vendor Names, RFP issued, Number of Vendors, Incument
column5: Known competitors, relevant incuments, or vendor actions. Raw inputs only; threat evaluation happens downstream.

column2: Last interaction timing, Next step defined or not, Buyer responsiveness (fast/normal/slow), Stakeholder attendance (single/multiple/new), Buyer-initiated actions
column5: Seller-provided summary of recent buyer interactions and engagement signals. Not a system log or integration; used to capture current interaction state (momentum, responsiveness, stakeholder involvement) for signal evaluation.
Indicates whether and how broadly the product is currently used. Descriptive only; does not imply deal maturity or expansion potential.
Describes how deeply the product is embedded in workflows. Input only; business impact and expansion viability evaluated downstream.

column2: Revenue growth, Cost reduction, Risk mitigation, Efficiency gain, Transformation
column5: Defines the future business outcomes or success criteria as stated by the buyer. Raw input only; quality and alignment evaluated downstream.

column2: Percentage improvement, Cost savings amount, Time reduction, Revenue increase, metrics
column5: Quantified or measurable impact tied to the problem or outcomes. Capture only what is explicitly stated; do not infer.

column2: None, Single stakeholder, Multiple stakeholders, Cross-functional
column5: Indicates breadth of stakeholder involvement. Input is descriptive only; alignment and influence evaluated downstream.
Whether the Economic Buyer (discretionary spend authority) has been identified. Do not infer based on title alone.
Indicates level of direct engagement with the Economic Buyer. Raw input only; commitment evaluated downstream.
Indicates whether a specific individual is responsible for driving evaluation, expansion, or internal coordination of the initiative.
Indicates whether the buyer is aware of pricing, licensing, or the need for a commercial agreement. Does not imply willingness to buy.
Indicates direction of product usage over time. Descriptive only; impact evaluated downstream.
Indicates whether workflows depend on the product. Input only; importance evaluated downstream.

column2: No impact, Minor disruption, Significant disruption
column5: Describes expected impact if the product is removed. Capture stated or observable impact only; do not infer.
